conceptCards.title: What is gravitational force?
conceptCards.description: Gravitational force is the attractive force between masses. Bigger masses create stronger pull, and distance weakens that pull.

conceptCards.title: Why do things fall?
conceptCards.description: Near a planet, gravity pulls objects toward the center of that planet. That downward pull creates acceleration.

conceptCards.title: What changes in this simulation?
conceptCards.description: When gravity increases, the object speeds up faster, reaches the ground sooner, and hits with greater speed.

learningPoints.question: Mass vs weight
learningPoints.answer: Mass is how much matter an object has. Weight is the force of gravity acting on that mass, so weight changes from planet to planet.

learningPoints.question: Acceleration due to gravity
learningPoints.answer: The value shown in m/s² tells you how much speed an object gains each second while falling.

learningPoints.question: Bounce in the simulation
learningPoints.answer: Bounce is not gravity itself. It represents how much motion stays after a collision with the floor.

faqItems.question: Do heavier objects always fall faster?
faqItems.answer: In ideal free fall without air resistance, objects accelerate at the same rate under the same gravity.

faqItems.question: Why is Jupiter gravity so high here?
faqItems.answer: Jupiter is much more massive than Earth, so the gravitational pull near it is much stronger.

faqItems.question: Why does distance matter?
faqItems.answer: The farther you are from a mass, the weaker the gravitational force becomes.

faqItems.question: Why can I enter custom gravity?
faqItems.answer: It lets you imagine motion on fictional planets or compare different gravitational environments.

props.title: Understand gravitational force with a live simulation
props.description: Learn what gravity is, why objects fall, and how stronger or weaker gravity changes motion. Use the playground to compare planets and see the force in action.

items.label: 10 kg weight

props.className: mx-auto max-w-7xl px-4 py-10 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 lg:py-16

props.title: What gravitational force means
props.description: Gravity is a force of attraction between masses. Near a planet, it pulls objects downward and gives them acceleration.

props.content: Stronger gravity means more acceleration every second. That is why objects fall faster on worlds with larger gravity values.

props.title: Mass, weight, and acceleration
props.description: These ideas are related, but they are not the same thing.

props.title: Mass
props.description: Mass measures how much matter an object has. It does not change when you move from one planet to another.

props.title: Weight
props.description: Weight is gravitational force acting on mass. For a 10 kg object here, the weight is {{weightOfTenKg}} newtons.

props.title: Acceleration
props.description: The current gravity of {{round(activeGravity * 100) / 100}} m/s² means the object's speed increases by about that much every second while it falls.

props.className: mx-auto max-w-7xl px-4 pb-10 sm:px-6 lg:px-8

props.title: Experiment with gravity
props.description: Change the environment and immediately see how the motion changes.

props.label: Planet preset
props.hint: Choose a planet or switch to custom gravity.

props.label: Custom gravity
props.hint: Enter any positive value in m/s².

props.label: Drop height
props.hint: Higher drops take longer and end with greater speed.

props.label: Bounce
props.hint: Controls how energetic the collision looks.

props.content: This setting is about {{earthPercent}}% of Earth's gravity.

props.title: See gravitational force in motion
props.description: The objects below fall under the selected gravity, so the visual behavior changes as the force changes.

props.className: mx-auto max-w-7xl px-4 pb-10 sm:px-6 lg:px-8

props.title: How gravity changes across worlds
props.description: The same object behaves differently because the gravitational acceleration is different on each world.

props.content: A higher m/s² value means stronger pull and quicker acceleration. A lower value means slower falling and a more floaty experience.

props.className: mx-auto max-w-7xl px-4 pb-10 sm:px-6 lg:px-8

props.title: Key takeaway
props.description: Gravity is a force that pulls masses together, and near a planet it causes falling motion.

props.title: What the number means
props.description: The gravity value in m/s² tells you how much speed is added every second during free fall.

props.title: Why simulations help
props.description: A live scene makes the invisible force easier to understand because you can connect the number directly to motion.

props.className: mx-auto max-w-7xl px-4 py-10 sm:px-6 lg:px-8

props.title: Concepts to remember
props.description: These points help connect the formula ideas to the motion you see.

props.title: Common questions
props.description: Short answers to doubts learners often have about gravity.
